title: Which Is Not A Goal Of The Hazcom Standard
source_filename: Which Is Not A Goal Of The Hazcom Standard.docx

qas.q:
The OSHA HAZCOM Standard requires employers to perform which of the following?

A) Ensure HM containers are labeled
B) Maintains a list of HM (referred to by the Navy as authorized use list)
C) Maintain a written HAZCOM Program
D) All of them

Which of the following best describes corrosives?

A) A corrosive is a chemical that causes harmless, warm sensation upon contact.
B) A chemical that glows in the dark.
C) A chemical that registers -14 on pH scale.
D) A corrosive is a chemical that causes visible destruction of, or irreversible damage to living tissue at the point of contact.
qas.a: D) A corrosive is a chemical that causes visible destruction of, or irreversible damage to living tissue at the point of contact.

qas.q:
__________________ facilities are required to be in areas where employees' eyes may be exposed to corrosive materials which are addressed in the SDS for the chemical.

A) Dining
B) Swimming
C) Emergency eyewash
D) None of the above

T or F: First Aid Measures are not required on an SDS.
A) True
B) False

The Standard was changed to provide additional benefits by compliance with the UN Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals (GHS) - Which of the choices below is not a benefit?
A) Increase the quality and consistency of information
B) Reduce confusion/increase comprehension of hazards
C) Facilitate training
D) None of the above
Which of the following Pictograms represents a cancer causing material

In the Accidental Release Section it covers all of the following except:
A) PPE
B) Emergency procedures
C) Where the drains are located
D) Cleanup procedures
qas.a: C) Where the drains are located

qas.q:
Potential cancer causing agents within a material are addressed in which Section of the SDS sheet?
A) Ecological Information
B) Stability and Reactivity
C) Toxicological Information
D) Composition/Information on Ingredients

T or F: We changed from Safety Data Sheet to Material Safety Data Sheets with this update to the Standard.
A) True
B) False

Some types of HM, like mercury, can be absorbed directly into the bloodstream through this route of exposure.
A) Ingestion
B) Eye Contact
C) Inhalation
D) Skin Absorption

"Readily accessible" means?
A) Easy to get at except during a power failure
B) MUST be on paper
C) immediate access regardless of the situation
D) Orally over the phone
qas.a: C) immediate access regardless of the situation

qas.q:
Which is not a goal of the HAZCOM Standard?
A) Establish uniform requirements for communication of hazardous chemical information to both the employer and the employee
B) Address literacy problems
C) Reduce injury/illness caused by hazardous chemicals in the workplace.
D) Identify and evaluate chemical hazards.

Which of the following is NOT Chemical Manufacturers Responsibility?
A) Conduct hazard determinations
B) Update SDSs as needed
C) Inform and train workers
D) Obtain/prepare material safety data sheets (SDSs)

The OSHA HAZCOM Standard requires employers to perform which of the following?
A) Ensure HM containers are labeled
B) Maintain a list of HM (referred to by the Navy as authorized use list)
C) Maintain a written HAZCOM Program
D) All of the above

Which of the following represents the basic warning found on hazard warning labels?
A) Danger: Highly Toxic
B) Warning: Moderately Toxic
C) Caution: Slightly Toxic
D) A or B

Each workplace is responsible for ensuring that hazard warning labels contain all of the following EXCEPT:
A) Manufacturer's name
B) Product name
C) Hazard warning
D) Label must glow in the dark
qas.a: D) Label must glow in the dark

qas.q:
How many Sections must the SDS have?
A) 10 Sections
B) 12 Sections
C) 14 Sections
D) 16 Sections

How is an AUL different from a list of hazardous chemicals?
A) They are the same
B) The AUL is what is allowed in the workplace
C) One is a list of chemicals, and the other is a list of products
D) The AUL is not a list
qas.a: B) The AUL is what is allowed in the workplace

qas.q:
Which color does National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) and the National Paint and Coating Associations Hazardous Materials Identification System (HMIS) use for FLAMMABILITY?
A) Blue
B) Yellow
C) Red
D) White

Which statement regarding compressed gas cylinders is TRUE?
A) The gases are stored under great pressure
B) Gas cylinders pose no risk
C) Gas cylinders can withstand 3000 degrees Celsuis before blowing up.
D) None of the above
qas.a: A) The gases are stored under great pressure

qas.q:
Which of the following is not required on labels
A) The ingredients
B) Signal Words
C) Pictogram
D) Precautionary statements

Which statement regarding acute exposure is TRUE?
A) effects often develop slowly
B) worker may be unaware of exposure
C) exposures are typically sudden and severe
D) None of the above
qas.a: C) exposures are typically sudden and severe

qas.q:
Liquids that evaporates, gases, dusts, fumes or mists are measured in:
A) Megatons
B) Gigawatts
C) parts per million (ppm) and milligrams per cubic meter (mg/m3)
D) Brake Horse Power (bhp)
qas.a: C) parts per million (ppm) and milligrams per cubic meter (mg/m3)

qas.q:
Which of the following best describes toxic substances?
A) substance which effect either male or female reproductive system and may impair the ability to have children.
B) chemicals which have been determined through experience or laboratory analysis to have lethal effects to humans based on dosage criteria.
C) agents which act on the blood which decreases the hemoglobin function and deprives the body tissue or oxygen.
D) substance which causes the development of cancerous growths in living tissue, whether actually known, or through experiments with animals.
qas.a: B) chemicals which have been determined through experience or laboratory analysis to have lethal effects to humans based on dosage criteria.

qas.q:
Examples of hazardous chemicals categories with their associated physical hazards include all of the following EXCEPT:
A) compressed gas
B) explosives
C) Spent shell casings
D) water-reactive materials
